stem: Maria R. (retail SMB owner) just said: "Yeah, that does sound interesting. Send me something?" You have Calendar open in your second tab. Which close fires the L4 proxy Next_Step_Booked__c = TRUE during this call?

options.label: A
options.text: "I'll send a calendar invite by email later today."
options.rationale: Partial · GC-05 pattern. The flag will not flip until you actually send it, and "later today" is where the deal slips. Top reps send during the call.

options.label: B
options.text: "What's the best 20 minutes next week to walk you through the SMB version — Tuesday 11 or Thursday 2?"
options.rationale: Yes. Two specific slots, one sentence, sent during the call. M.G.'s GC-01 pattern · Salesforce flag flips before you hang up.

options.label: C
options.text: "Let me know which day works best for you."
options.rationale: Open-ended close. Puts the work on the buyer · no two-slot anchor · GC-21 pattern.

options.label: D
options.text: "I'll follow up next week with some options."
options.rationale: GC-10 / GC-22 anti-pattern. "Follow up" is where deals go to die. No re-engage in the sample data.

stem: A peer rep tells you: "I always pick the slots after the call ends — I want to double-check my own calendar first." What is the cost of that habit in the Gong + Salesforce data?

options.label: A
options.text: None — the buyer accepted, so the booking is the same.
options.rationale: No. The booking may eventually happen, but the same-call Next_Step_Booked__c flag stays FALSE, and re-engage drops to the GC-10 / GC-11 base rate (low).

options.label: B
options.text: The Salesforce flag stays FALSE that day, and re-engage drops to the GC-10 / GC-11 base rate.
options.rationale: Yes. "After the call" is the failure mode by another name. The L4 proxy is binary — set during call, or not at all.

options.label: C
options.text: It is fine if the rep sends the invite within 24 hours.
options.rationale: Not according to the Gong sample. GC-11 sent the invite the next morning and still never re-engaged.

options.label: D
options.text: It is better — checking the calendar twice prevents double-booking.
options.rationale: Calendar conflicts can be resolved by re-sending. Cold re-engage cannot. The cost is asymmetric.

stem: You read out two slots, the buyer says "Tuesday 11 works." What is the very next thing you do, still on the call?

options.label: A
options.text: Thank them and end the call — confirmation by email tomorrow.
options.rationale: Anti-pattern. Confirmation must happen now · the buyer's acceptance is the cue to send, not delay.

options.label: B
options.text: Click "Send invite" in Calendar and watch Next_Step_Booked__c flip to TRUE in Salesforce.
options.rationale: Yes. Send → flag flips → confirm with the buyer that the invite is in their inbox. All three steps before you hang up.

options.label: C
options.text: Ask whether they want a reminder 1 day before.
options.rationale: Reminder cadence is nice-to-have. The send-during-call is non-negotiable · order matters.

options.label: D
options.text: Pivot to a second pitch prop while you have their attention.
options.rationale: Tempting, but you risk losing the booking. Lock the next step first, then pitch on the booked call.
